response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a seasoned full-stack engineer with deep TypeScript/Node.js expertise, but the role demands 5–10 years of hands-on C++ on Windows with capital-markets domain knowledge — neither of which he demonstrates. His C++ exposure amounts to roughly six months at National Instruments and a bachelor thesis project, with no indication of Windows-platform work or financial context. His only financial-adjacent role (RIDE Capital) is consumer fintech rather than capital markets or fixed income, and his database experience centres on MongoDB and MySQL rather than SQL Server or Oracle.
strengths: Strong software-craftsmanship fundamentals (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development) demonstrated consistently across multiple employers
Java background satisfies one of the listed desirable skills
Demonstrated ability to work in high-throughput, low-latency distributed systems at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic
concerns: C++ experience is negligible (~6 months at National Instruments plus a student thesis project), far short of the 5–10 year mandatory requirement
No capital markets, fixed income, or investment banking domain exposure; RIDE Capital is consumer investment fintech, not capital markets
No evidence of SQL Server or Oracle experience; database work is primarily MongoDB and MySQL
